  r.store = store\n251\t    return r\n252\t\n253\t\n254\tdef _make_router_with_response(router, response_text):\n255\t    \"\"\"Wire a mock ZAI client onto the router that returns response_text.\"\"\"\n256\t    mock_client = AsyncMock()\n257\t    # The router expects call_simple to return a dict with content and timing\n258\t    mock_response = {\n259\t        \"content\": router._zai_client = mock_client\n265\t    router._router_zai_client = mock_client  # Also mock the dedicated router client\n266\t    return router\n267\t\n268\t\n269\t# --- 1. classification parsing ---------------------------------------------\n270\t\n271\tclass TestIntentClassification:\n272\t    \"\"\"Each pre-canned utterance classifies to its expected IntentType.\"\"\"\n273\t\n274\t    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\n275\t        \"utterance,canned_response,expected_type\",\n276\t        CANNED_CLASSIFICATIONS,\n277\t        ids=[c[2].value for c in CANNED_CLASSIFICATIONS],\n278\t    )\n279\t    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n280\t    async def test_utterance_classifies_to_expected_intent(\n281\t        self, router, utterance, canned_response, expected_type\n282\t    ):\n283\t        r = == 1\n288\t        assert classifications[0].intent_type == expected_type\n289\t        # The fragment is propagated from the canned response, not dropped.\n290\t        assert classifications[0].utterance_fragment\n291\t\n292\t    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n293\t    async def test_classification_preserves_project_slug_and_urgency(self, router):\n294\t        \"\"\"project_slug and urgency from the LLM response reach the object.\"\"\"\n295\t        canned = json.dumps([{\n296\t            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n297\t            \"project_slug\": -------------------------------------\n402\t\n403\tclass TestIntentToFetchCommandMapping:\n404\t    \"\"\"The router maps each intent to the correct fetch source set.\"\"\"\n405\t\n406\t    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES))\n407\t    def test_map_intent_type_resolves_correctly(self, intent_type):\n408\t        \"\"\"_map_intent_type maps router intent_type):\n415\t        \"\"\"\n416\t        For each fetchable intent, the source set returned by\n417\t        get_fetch_commands matches the matrix (order-independent). A divergence\n418\t        means an intent fetches the wrong — or no — sources.\n419\t        \"\"\"\n420\t        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n421\t        specs = get_fetch_commands(mapped)\n422\t        actual_sources = {spec.source for spec in specs}\n423\t\n424\t        assert actual_sources == EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES[intent_type], (\n425\t            f\"Source set for {intent_type.value} diverged from expected\"\n426\t        )\n427\t\n428\t    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_REQUIRED_SOURCES))\n429\t    def test_required_sources_match_matrix(self, intent_type):\n430\t        \"\"\"Required sources for each intent match the matrix's required=True set.\"\"\"\n431\t        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n432\t        required = set(get_required_sources(mapped))\n433\t\n434\t        assert required == EXPECTED_REQUIRED_SOURCES[intent_type], (\n435\t            f\"Required sources for {intent_type.value} diverged from expected\"\n436\t        )\n437\t\n438\t    def test_expected_sets_are_consistent_with_matrix_definition(self):\n439\t        \"\"\"\n440\t        Guard against the EXPECTED_* dicts and FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX drifting\n441\t        apart silently: every fetchable intent present in the matrix must have\n442\t        an expected entry, and vice-versa.\n443\t\n444\t        Compared on .value strings because FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX is keyed by the\n445\t        fetch IntentType while EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES is keyed by the router\n446\t        IntentType — sibling enums that share value strings (the same contract\n447\t        _map_intent_type relies on) but are distinct classes.\n448\t        \"\"\"\n449\t        matrix_values = {t.value for t in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX}\n450\t        expected_values = {t.value for t in EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES}\n451\t\n452\t        # TASK_PROFILE/CLARIFICATION are intentionally absent from EXPECTED_*\n453\t        # (TASK_PROFILE escalates instead of fetching; CLARIFICATION is a\n454\t        # non-dispatched meta-type). Every *other* matrix intent must be covered.\n455\t        assert expected_values == matrix_values, (\n456\t            \"EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES does not cover exactly the fetch matrix\"\n457\t        )\n458\t\n459\t    def test_action_intent_has_required_sources_others_optional(self):\n460\t        \"\"\"\n461\t        ACTION is the canonical 'required source' intent: its required sources\n462\t        are a proper non-empty subset of its full source set, and the\n463\t        orchestrator will treat their failure as blocking.\n464\t        \"\"\"\n465\t        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(IntentType.ACTION)\n466\t        full = {s.source for s in get_fetch_commands(mapped)}\n467\t        required = set(get_required_sources(mapped))\n468\t\n469\t        assert required  # Second call: cache hit, no ZAI call\n707\t        classifications2, timing2 = await r.classify_utterance(\n708\t            \"check pods\", \"session-123\"\n709\t        )\n710\t        assert len(classifications2) == 1\n711\t        assert timing2[\"cached\"] is True\n712\t        assert timing2[\"total_ms\"] == 0\n713\t\n714\t        # Entries older than TTL are treated\n781\t        as misses and removed.\n782\t        \"\"\"\n783\t        import time\n784\t        from unittest.mock import patch\n785\t\n786\t        canned = json.dumps([{\n787\t            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n788\t            \"utterance_fragment\": \"test\",\n789\t        }])\n790\t        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n791\t\n792\t        # First call: populate cache\n793\t        await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n794\t\n795\t        # Mock time.time() to simulate 301 seconds later (beyond 300s TTL)\n796\t        with patch('time.time', return_value=time.time() + 301):\n797\t            # Should be a cache miss due to TTL expiration\n798\t            _, timing = await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n799\t            assert timing[\"cached\"] is False\n800\t\n801\t    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n802\t    async def test_cache_stats_logged_periodically(self, router, caplog):\n803\t        \"\"\"\n804\t        Cache statistics are logged every 50 requests (by default). TestIntentToFetchCommandMapping:\n    \"\"\"The router maps each intent to the correct fetch source set.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES))\n    def test_map_intent_type_resolves_correctly(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"_map_intent_type maps router IntentType → fetch IntentType.\"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n        mapped = router._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        assert mapped == FetchIntentType(intent_type.value)\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES))\n    def test_fetch_command_source_set_matches_matrix(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"\n        For each fetchable intent, the source set returned by\n        get_fetch_commands matches the matrix (order-independent). A divergence\n        means an intent fetches the wrong — or no — sources.\n        \"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        specs = get_fetch_commands(mapped)\n        actual_sources = {spec.source for spec in specs}\n\n        assert actual_sources == EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES[intent_type], (\n            f\"Source set for {intent_type.value} diverged from expected\"\n        )\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_REQUIRED_SOURCES))\n    def test_required_sources_match_matrix(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"Required sources for each intent match the matrix's required=True set.\"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        required = set(get_required_sources(mapped))\n\n        assert required == EXPECTED_REQUIRED_SOURCES[intent_type], (\n            f\"Required sources for {intent_type.value} diverged from expected\"\n        )\n\n    def test_expected_sets_are_consistent_with_matrix_definition(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Guard against the EXPECTED_* dicts and FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX drifting\n        apart silently: every fetchable intent present in the matrix must have\n        an expected entry, and vice-versa.\n\n        Compared on .value strings because FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX is keyed by the\n        fetch IntentType while EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES is keyed by the router\n        IntentType — sibling enums that share value strings (the same contract\n        _map_intent_type relies on) but are distinct classes.\n        \"\"\"\n        matrix_values = {t.value for t in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX}\n        expected_values = {t.value for t in EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES}\n\n        # TASK_PROFILE/CLARIFICATION are intentionally absent from EXPECTED_*\n        # (TASK_PROFILE escalates instead of fetching; CLARIFICATION is a\n        # non-dispatched meta-type). Every *other* matrix intent must be covered.\n        assert expected_values == matrix_values, (\n            \"EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES does not cover exactly the fetch matrix\"\n        )\n\n    def test_action_intent_has_required_sources_others_optional(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        ACTION is the canonical 'required source' intent: its required sources\n        are a proper non-empty subset of its full source set, and the\n        orchestrator will treat their failure as blocking.\n        \"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(IntentType.ACTION)\n        full = {s.source for s in get_fetch_commands(mapped)}\n        required = set(get_required_sources(mapped))\n\n        assert required  # non-empty\n        assert required.issubset(full)  # required ⊆ full\n        assert FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS in required  # the action-specific source\n\n\n# --- 5. meta-type handling: TASK_PROFILE & CLARIFICATION --------------------\n\nclass TestMetaTypeHandling:\n    \"\"\"\n    TASK_PROFILE and CLARIFICATION are not dispatched to the fetch pipeline:\n    TASK_PROFILE escalates to a NEEDLE bead, CLARIFICATION awaits user input.\n    Neither should resolve fetch commands.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_task_profile_classifies_but_does_not_fetch(self, router):\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"task-profile\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"implement the digest feature\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.9,\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification], _ = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"implement the digest feature\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        # Classified correctly...\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE\n        # ...but its string value is not a key in the fetch matrix, so it would\n        # never resolve real fetch sources (process_intent escalates instead).\n        mapped = r._map_intent_type(IntentType.TASK_PROFILE)\n        # The default fallback is STATUS, proving TASK_PROFILE has no dedicated\n        # fetch mapping of its own.\n        assert mapped == FetchIntentType.STATUS\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_clarification_is_not_a_fetch_intent(self, router):\n        \"\"\"CLARIFICATION is a meta-type with no dedicated fetch mapping.\"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"what?\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.3,\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification], _ = await r.classify_utterance(\"what?\", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.CLARIFICATION\n        # Its value is absent from the fetch matrix.\n        assert classification.intent_type.value not in {\n            t.value for t in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX\n        }\n\n\n# --- 6. malformed-response fallback ----------------------------------------\n\nclass TestFallbackOnMalformedResponse:\n    \"\"\"A non-JSON LLM response must degrade to a single STATUS intent, not raise.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_unparseable_response_defaults_to_status(self, router):\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, \"this is not json at all\")\n\n        classifications = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"something happened\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        assert len(classifications) == 1\n        assert classifications[0].intent_type == IntentType.STATUS\n        assert classifications[0].confidence < 0.6  # flagged as low-confidence\n\n\n# --- 7. edge cases: empty/ambiguous inputs (bead adc-5qdx) ------------------\n#\n# The classes above cover the happy path for every IntentType plus malformed\n# responses. This class closes the edge-case gap called out in the acceptance\n# criteria — empty strings, whitespace-only input, zero-intent responses, and\n# stripped-down LLM payloads missing optional fields. The ZAI client is still\n# mocked, so each case is deterministic: we feed the exact JSON envelope the\n# router would receive for that degenerate input and assert it degrades safely\n# rather than raising.\n\n\nclass TestEdgeCases:\n    \"\"\"Empty, whitespace, and ambiguous inputs must classify without raising.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_empty_string_utterance_classifies_to_clarification(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        An empty utterance must flow through classify_utterance without raising.\n        A realistic LLM response for empty input is a single low-confidence\n        clarification; the router must reproduce that verdict.\n        \"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.2,\n            \"reasoning\": \"empty utterance, nothing to route\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\"\", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.CLARIFICATION\n        assert classification.utterance_fragment == \"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_whitespace_only_utterance_does_not_crash(self, router):\n        \"\"\"Whitespace-only input is no different structurally from empty.\"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.1,\n            \"reasoning\": \"whitespace only\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\"   \\n\\t  \", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.CLARIFICATION\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_empty_intent_array_returns_empty_list(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        If the LLM segments the utterance into zero intents (empty JSON array),\n        the router returns an empty list — not None, not a crash, not a\n        synthesized fallback. Callers must tolerate [].\n        \"\"\"\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, \"[]\")\n\n        classifications, _ = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"anything — model returned no intents\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        assert classifications == []\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_missing_optional_fields_use_defaults(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        The router must tolerate a minimal payload containing only intent_type.\n        Every optional field falls back to a defined default rather than\n        KeyError/AttributeError. Locks the .get()-with-default contract in\n        classify_utterance against drift in the IntentClassification dataclass.\n        \"\"\"\n        utterance = \"show me the deploy status\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\"intent_type\": \"status\"}])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification], _ = await r.classify_utterance(utterance, \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.STATUS\n        assert classification.project_slug is None\n        assert classification.urgency == \"normal\"\n        assert classification.reasoning == \"\"\n        # confidence default in the parser is 0.8 (NOT the dataclass's 1.0).\n        assert classification.confidence == pytest.approx(0.8)\n        # fragment falls back to the full utterance when the LLM omits it.\n        assert classification.utterance_fragment == utterance\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_deeply_ambiguous_fragment_routes_to_clarification(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        Per prompts/router.md, confidence < 0.7 routes to clarification. A\n        maximally ambiguous fragment (\"hmm\") yields a clarification intent with\n        sub-threshold confidence — the router must surface that meta-type intact\n        rather than forcing a best-guess dispatch.\n        \"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"hmm\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.35,\n            \"reasoning\": \"no actionable signal in the utterance\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\"hmm\", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.CLARIFICATION\n        assert classification.confidence < 0.7\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_missing_intent_type_field_defaults_to_status(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        An intent object with no intent_type key at all must fall back to STATUS\n        (the parser's documented default) rather than raising — mirrors the\n        unknown-string fallback but for the field-absent case.\n        \"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\"utterance_fragment\": \"something\"}])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\"something\", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.STATUS\n\n\n# --- 8. cache behavior (bead adc-2qhmb) ---------------------------------------\n\nclass TestRouterCacheBehavior:\n    \"\"\"\n    Router response caching eliminates redundant ZAI proxy calls for similar\n    utterances. First call: cache miss, calls ZAI\n        classifications1, timing1 = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"check pods\", \"session-123\"\n        )\n        assert len(classifications1) == 1\n        assert timing1[\"cached\"] is False\n        assert timing1[\"total_ms\"] > 0\n\n        # Second call: cache hit, no ZAI call\n        classifications2, timing2 = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"check pods\", \"session-123\"\n        )\n        assert len(classifications2) == 1\n        assert timing2[\"cached\"] is True\n        assert timing2[\"total_ms\"] == 0\n\n        # First call: miss (cache was cleared by fixture, so stats start at 0)\n        await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n        assert router_module._cache_misses == 1\n        assert router_module._cache_hits == 0\n\n        # Second call: hit\n        await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n        assert router_module._cache_hits == 1\n        assert router_module._cache_misses == 1\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_ttl_is_five_minutes(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        Cache TTL is 5 minutes (300 seconds). Entries older than TTL are treated\n        as misses and removed.\n        \"\"\"\n        import time\n        from unittest.mock import patch\n\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"test\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        # First call: populate cache\n        await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n\n        # Mock time.time() to simulate 301 seconds later (beyond 300s TTL)\n        with patch('time.time', return_value=time.time() + 301):\n            # Should be a cache miss due to TTL expiration\n            _, timing = await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n            assert timing[\"cached\"] is False\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_stats_logged_periodically(self, router, caplog):\n        \"\"\"\n        Cache statistics are logged every 50 requests (by default). This test\n        verifies the periodic logging behavior.\n        \"\"\"\n        import logging\n        caplog.set_level(logging.INFO)\n\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"test\",\n        }]))\n\n        import src.intent.router as router_module\n\n        # Set stats so next request triggers logging (49 hits so far)\n        router_module._cache_hits = 49\n        router_module._cache_misses = 0\n\n        # This 50th request should trigger stats logging\n        with caplog.at_level(logging.INFO):\n            await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n\n        # Verify stats were logged (look for the stats log message)\n        assert any(\"Router cache statistics\" in record.message for record in caplog.records)\n        assert any(\"hit_rate=\" in record.message for record in caplog.records)\n","numLines":828,"startLine":1,"totalLines":828}}}

Use dedicated router client with 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n322\t            client = await self._get_router_zai_client()\n323\t\n324\t            # Measure prompt construction time (template rendering, message formatting)\n325\t            # This isolates client-side preparation cost from network and inference time\n326\t            prompt_start = time.perf_counter()\n327\t\n328\t            # Build user message\n329\t            user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}\"\n330\t\n331\t            logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n332\t\n333\t            # Build system prompt from prompts/router.md\n334\t            # (hot-reload enabled via reload manager)\n335\t            system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n336\t\n337\t            prompt_ms = (time.perf_counter() - prompt_start) * 1000\n338\t\n339\t            # Measure ZAI proxy call time (network + model inference)\n340\t            # The LLM client now returns timing breakdown separating network latency from model inference\n341\t            proxy_start = time.perf_counter()\n342\t            response_data = await client.call_simple(\n343\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n344\t                user_message=user_message,\n345\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n346\t                max_tokens=128,  # OPTIMIZATION 2: Reduced from 256 to 128 - intent JSON is typically 50-80 tokens\n347\t                temperature=0.0,\n348\t                return_timing=True,  # Request timing breakdown from LLM client\n349\t            )\n350\t            proxy_ms = (time.perf_counter() - proxy_start) * 1000\n351\t\n352\t            # Extract content and timing from response\n353\t            # response_data is now a dict with content and timing breakdown\n354\t            response = response_data.get(\"content\", \"\")\n355\t            timing_network_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_network_ms\")\n356\t            timing_inference_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_inference_ms\")\n357\t\n358\t            # Store raw response for error reporting\n359\t            raw_response = response\n360\t\n361\t            # Calculate model inference time: proxy response time - network time\n362\t            # This gives us the actual model inference duration by subtracting network RTT\n363\t            calculated_inference_ms = None\n364\t            if timing_network_ms is not None:\n365\t                calculated_inference_ms = proxy_ms - timing_network_ms\n366\t\n367\t            # Measure JSON parsing time\n368\t            parse_start = time.perf_counter()\n369\t            try:\n370\t                # Optimized JSON parsing using centralized parser\n371\t                # Uses single-pass find()/rfind() approach (faster than split/rsplit)\n372\t                # Handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced responses efficiently\n373\t                # See src/llm/response_parser.py for implementation\n374\t                intents_data = parse_llm_response(response, strip_fences=True, expect_json=True)\n375\t            except ParseLLMError as e:\n376\t                # Already has raw_response attached; just re-raise\n377\t                raise e\n378\t            parse_ms = (time.perf_counter() - parse_start) * 1000\n379\t\n380\t            # Measure classification processing time\n381\t            process_start = time.perf_counter()\n382\t            classifications = []\n383\t\n384\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n385\t                # Map string to IntentType enum\n386\t                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n387\t                try:\n388\t                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n389\t                except ValueError:\n390\t                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n391\t                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n392\t\n393\t                classification = IntentClassification(\n394\t                    intent_type=intent_type,\n395\t                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n396\t                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n397\t                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n398\t                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n399\t                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n400\t                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n401\t                )\n402\t                classifications.append(classification)\n403\t            process_ms = (time.perf_counter() - process_start) * 1000\n404\t\n405\t            total_ms = (time.perf_counter() - classify_start) * 1000\n406\t\n407\t            # Log detailed timing breakdown for latency profiling\n408\t            # This shows WHERE the 1,587-2,074ms latency is spent across the 4 investigation areas\n409\t            network_str = f\"{timing_network_ms:.2f}\" if timing_network_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n410\t            calculated_inference_str = f\"{calculated_inference_ms:.2f}\" if calculated_inference_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n411\t            logger.info(\n412\t                f\"router_timing breakdown: \"\n413\t                f\"prompt_construction_ms={prompt_ms:.2f} \"\n414\t                f\"proxy_call_ms={proxy_ms:.2f} \"\n415\t                f\"proxy_network_ms={network_str} \"\n416\t                f\"proxy_inference_ms={calculated_inference_str} \"\n417\t                f\"json_parse_ms={parse_ms:.2f} \"\n418\t                f\"process_ms={process_ms:.2f} \"\n419\t                f\"total_ms={total_ms:.2f} \"\n420\t                f\"intents={len(classifications)}\"\n421\t            )\n422\t\n423\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n424\t\n425\t            # Build timing breakdown for storage\n426\t            # Now includes separate network and inference timing measured by LLM client\n427\t            timing_breakdown = {\n428\t                \"cached\": False,  # Cache miss - result from fresh ZAI proxy call\n429\t                \"prompt_construction_ms\": round(prompt_ms, 2),\n430\t                \"proxy_call_ms\": round(proxy_ms, 2),  # Total round-trip time (network + inference)\n431\t                \"proxy_network_ms\": round(timing_network_ms, 2) if timing_network_ms is not None else None,\n432\t                \"proxy_inference_ms\": round(calculated_inference_ms, 2) if calculated_inference_ms is not None else None,\n433\t                \"json_parse_ms\": round(parse_ms, 2),\n434\t                \"process_ms\": round(process_ms, 2),\n435\t                \"total_ms\": round(total_ms, 2),\n436\t                \"intents_count\": len(classifications),\n437\t            }\n438\t\n439\t            # OPTIMIZATION 1 (cont): Cache successful classifications\n440\t            self._cache_classification(utterance, session_id, classifications)\n441\t\n442\t            # Log cache statistics periodically (including this cache miss)\n443\t            self._log_cache_stats_if_needed()\n444\t\n445\t            return classifications, timing_breakdown\n446\t\n447\t        except ParseLLMError as e:\n448\t            \"\"\"\n449\t            First parsing attempt failed - implement corrective retry.\n450\t\n451\t            Router uses corrective retry because it's the first step in the pipeline.\n452\t            A malformed router response cascades to everything downstream.\n453\t            One retry is justified because it's cheap (same prompt, 2048 tokens).\n454\t            \"\"\"\n455\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response: {e}\")\n456\t            raw_output = e.raw_response or raw_response  # Preserve for error reporting\n457\t\n458\t            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n459\t            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n460\t                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n461\t                retry_count += 1\n462\t                try:\n463\t                    # Retry once with same parameters\n464\t                    classifications, _ = await self.classify_utterance(\n465\t                        utterance=utterance,\n466\t                        session_id=session_id,\n467\t                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n468\t                    )\n469\t                    return classifications, _\n470\t                except ParseLLMError as retry_e:\n471\t                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n472\t                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n473\t                        parse_error=str(retry_e),\n474\t                        raw_output=retry_e.raw_response or raw_response,\n475\t                        retry_count=retry_count,\n476\t                    ) from retry_e\n477\t\n478\t            # No retry allowed or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n479\t            raise RouterMalformedError(\n480\t                parse_error=str(e),\n481\t                raw_output=raw_output,\n482\t                retry_count=retry_count,\n483\t            ) from e\n484\t\n485\t        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n486\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n487\t            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n488\t\n489\t        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n490\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n491\t            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n492\t\n493\t        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n494\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n495\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n496\t\n497\t        except Exception as e:\n498\t            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n499\t            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n500\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n501\t\n502\t    async def route_utterance(\n503\t        self,\n504\t        utterance: str,\n505\t        utterance_id: str,\n506\t        session_id: str,\n507\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n508\t        \"\"\"\n509\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n510\t\n511\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n512\t\n513\t        Args:\n514\t            utterance: The user utterance\n515\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n516\t            session_id: Session ID\n517\t\n518\t        Returns:\n519\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\n520\t\n521\t        Raises:\n522\t            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n523\t        \"\"\"\n524\t        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n525\t        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n526\t\n527\t        try:\n528\t            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n529\t            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n530\t            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n531\t            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n532\t            classifications, timing_breakdown = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n533\t            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n534\t\n535\t            # Store router timing breakdown in utterance record\n536\t            try:\n537\t                store = await self._get_store()\n538\t                await store.update_utterance_router_timing(utterance_id, timing_breakdown)\n539\t                logger.info(f\"Stored router timing breakdown for utterance {utterance_id[:8]}\")\n540\t            except Exception as e:\n541\t                logger.warning(f\"Failed to store router timing breakdown: {e}\")\n542\t                # Non-fatal: continue with routing\n543\t\n544\t            routed_intents = []\n545\t            for classification in classifications:\n546\t                # Create intent ID\n547\t                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n548\t\n549\t                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n550\t                    intent_id=intent_id,\n551\t                    classification=classification,\n552\t                    session_id=session_id,\n553\t                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n554\t                    router_ms=router_ms,\n555\t                )\n556\t                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n557\t\n558\t            return routed_intents\n559\t\n560\t        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n561\t            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n562\t            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n563\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n564\t            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n565\t                utterance=utterance,\n566\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n567\t                session_id=session_id,\n568\t                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n569\t                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n570\t                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n571\t            )\n572\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n573\t\n574\t        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n575\t            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n576\t            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n577\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n578\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n579\t                utterance=utterance,\n580\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n581\t                session_id=session_id,\n582\t                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n583\t            )\n584\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n585\t\n586\t        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n587\t            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n588\t            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n589\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n590\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n591\t                utterance=utterance,\n592\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n593\t                session_id=session_id,\n594\t                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n595\t            )\n596\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n597\t\n598\t        except RouterProxyError as e:\n599\t            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n600\t            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n601\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n602\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n603\t                utterance=utterance,\n604\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n605\t                session_id=session_id,\n606\t                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n607\t            )\n608\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n609\t\n610\t    async def process_intent(\n611\t        self,\n612\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n613\t    ) -> dict:\n614\t        \"\"\"\n615\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n616\t\n617\t        Args:\n618\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n619\t\n620\t        Returns:\n621\t            Result dictionary with status and data\n622\t        \"\"\"\n623\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n624\t\n625\t        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n626\t        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n627\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n628\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n629\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n630\t        # the dispatch itself.\n631\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n632\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n633\t\n634\t        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n635\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n636\t            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n637\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n638\t            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n639\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n640\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n641\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n642\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n643\t            )\n644\t\n645\t            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n646\t            store = await self._get_store()\n647\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n648\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n649\t                \"resolved\",\n650\t                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n651\t            )\n652\t\n653\t            return {\n654\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n655\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n656\t                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n657\t                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n658\t            }\n659\t\n660\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n661\t            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n662\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n663\t            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n664\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n665\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n666\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n667\t            )\n668\t\n669\t            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n670\t            store = await self._get_store()\n671\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n672\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n673\t                \"resolved\",\n674\t                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n675\t            )\n676\t\n677\t            return {\n678\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n679\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n680\t                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n681\t                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n682\t            }\n683\t\n684\t        try:\n685\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n686\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n687\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n688\t            else:\n689\t                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n690\t                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n691\t        except Exception:\n692\t            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n693\t            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n694\t            raise\n695\t\n696\t        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n697\t        return result\n698\t\n699\t    async def _persist_timings(\n700\t        self,\n701\t        intent_id: str,\n702\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n703\t    ) -> None:\n704\t        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n705\t        try:\n706\t            store = await self._get_store()\n707\t            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n708\t        except Exception as e:\n709\t            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n710\t\n711\t    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n712\t        self,\n713\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n714\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n715\t    ) -> dict:\n716\t        \"\"\"\n717\t        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n718\t\n719\t        Args:\n720\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n721\t            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n722\t                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n723\t\n724\t        Returns:\n725\t            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n726\t        \"\"\"\n727\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n728\t\n729\t        logger.info(\n730\t            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n731\t            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n732\t        )\n733\t\n734\t        try:\n735\t            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n736\t            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(\n737\t                classification.intent_type,\n738\t                classification.lookup_kind\n739\t            )\n740\t\n741\t            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n742\t            from ..registry import get_project\n743\t            repo_path = None\n744\t            ssh_target = None\n745\t            host_alias = None\n746\t            registry = get_registry()\n747\t            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n748\t                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n749\t                if entry:\n750\t                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n751\t                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n752\t                    host_alias = entry.host\n753\t                    location = entry.display_path\n754\t                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n755\t                else:\n756\t                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n757\t\n758\t            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n759\t            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n760\t            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n761\t            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n762\t            project_cfg = (\n763\t                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n764\t                if classification.project_slug else None\n765\t            )\n766\t\n767\t            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n768\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n769\t                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n770\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n771\t                context=FetchContext(\n772\t                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n773\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n774\t                    repo_path=repo_path,\n775\t                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n776\t                    host_alias=host_alias,\n777\t                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n778\t                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n779\t                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n780\t                ),\n781\t            )\n782\t\n783\t            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n784\t            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n785\t            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n786\t            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n787\t            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n788\t            total_sources = len(get_fetch_commands(fetch_intent_type))\n789\t            completed_sources: list[int] = [0]\n790\t\n791\t            def _on_fetch_progress(source, result) -> None:\n792\t                \"\"\"Track fetch timing and broadcast progress events to pending card.\"\"\"\n793\t                # Track first source timing for fetch_first_source_ms metric\n794\t                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n795\t                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n796\t\n797\t                # Increment completed count\n798\t                completed_sources[0] += 1\n799\t\n800\t                # Broadcast progress event to canvas ('3/5 sources in')\n801\t                asyncio.create_task(broadcast_fetch_progress(\n802\t                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n803\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n804\t                    completed=completed_sources[0],\n805\t                    total=total_sources,\n806\t                    source_name=source.value,\n807\t                    source_status=result.status,\n808\t                ))\n809\t\n810\t            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_fetch_progress)\n811\t            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n812\t            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n813\t                timings.record(\n814\t                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n815\t                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n816\t                )\n817\t\n818\t            # Check for terminal failure: ALL sources failed (Degraded-State UX)\n819\t            if fetch_result.terminal_failure == \"all_sources_failed\":\n820\t                logger.error(\n821\t                    f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: \"\n822\t                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.failed)} failed, \"\n823\t                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.timed_out)} timed out\"\n824\t                )\n825\t\n826\t                # Build failed sources list for error event\n827\t                failed_sources = []\n828\t                for source, result in fetch_result.sources.items():\n829\t                    failed_sources.append({\n830\t                        \"source\": source.value,\n831\t                        \"status\": result.status,\n832\t                        \"error\": result.error,\n833\t                    })\n834\t\n835\t                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n836\t                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n837\t                await handler.broadcast_all_sources_failed(\n838\t                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n839\t                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n840\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n841\t                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n842\t                    failed_sources=failed_sources,\n843\t                )\n844\t\n845\t                # Update intent status to failed\n846\t                store = get_store()\n847\t                await store.update_intent_status(\n848\t                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n849\t                    \"failed\",\n850\t                    \"All fetch sources failed\",\n851\t                )\n852\t\n853\t                return {\n854\t                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n855\t                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n856\t                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n857\t                    \"error\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n858\t                    \"message\": \"No data — all required sources failed\",\n859\t                    \"terminal_failure\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n860\t                }\n861\t\n862\t            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n863\t            required_sources_failed = [\n864\t                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n865\t                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n866\t                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n867\t            ]\n868\t\n869\t            if required_sources_failed:\n870\t                failure_reason = f\"Required data sources failed: {', '.join(s.value for s in required_sources_failed)}\"\n871\t                logger.error(f\"Required sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n872\t\n873\t                # Handle terminal failure\n874\t                await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n875\t                    routed_intent=routed_intent,\n876\t                    failure_reason=failure_reason,\n877\t                    error_type=\"required_source_failure\",\n878\t                )\n879\t\n880\t                return {\n881\t                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n882\t                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n883\t                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n884\t                    \"error\": failure_reason,\n885\t                    \"message\": \"Required sources failed - cannot proceed\",\n886\t                }\n887\t\n888\t            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n889\t            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n890\t project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n894\t                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n895\t                urgency=classification.urgency,\n896\t            )\n897\t\n898\t            synth_start = timings.clock()\n899\t            try:\n900\t                synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n901\t            except Exception as synth_e:\n902\t                # ZAI failure at synthesize stage - broadcast degraded_raw_data\n903\t                # (Degraded-State UX: fetched data is never discarded)\n904\t                logger.error(\n905\t                    f\"Synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {synth_e}\"\n906\t                )\n907\t\n908\t                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n909\t                await error_reason=str(synth_e),\n916\t                )\n917\t\n918\t                # Update intent status to failed\n919\t                store = get_store()\n920\t                await )\n925\t\n926\t                return {\n927\t                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n928\t                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n929\t                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n930\t                    \"error\": \"synthesize_failed\",\n931\t                    \"message\": \"Summary unavailable — showing raw fetch data\",\n932\t                    \"degraded\": True,\n933\t                }\n934\t\n935\t            timings.record(\"synthesize_total_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(synth_start))\n936\t            # synthesize_first_token_ms is not         }\n1001\t\n1002\t        except Exception as e:\n1003\t            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n1004\t\n1005\t            # Detect           \"message\": \"Fetch/synthesize failed - terminal error\",\n1018\t            }\n1019\t\n1020\t    async def _handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n1021\t        self,\n1022\t   IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n1069\t            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n1070\t            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n1071\t           return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_DOCS\n1085\t\n1086\t        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n1087\t\n1088\t    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n1089\t      }\n1259\t\n1260\t    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n1261\t        self,\n1262\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n1263\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n1264\t    ) -> dict:\n1265\t        \"\"\"\n1266\t        Escalate a task-profile intent to a NEEDLESS bead.\n1267\t\n1268\t        Args:\n1269\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to escalate\n1270\t            timings:          \"message\": \"Failed to escalate to bead\",\n1370\t            }\n1371\t\n1372\t\n1373\t# Global router instance\n1374\t_router: Optional[IntentRouter] = None\n1375\t\n1376\t\n1377\tdef get_router(store=None) -> IntentRouter:\n1378\t    \"\"\"Get or create the global intent router instance.\"\"\"\n1379\t    global _router\n1380\t    if _router is None:\n1381\t        _router = IntentRouter(store=store)\n1382\t    return _router\n1383\t\n1384\t\n1385\tdef clear_router_cache() -> None:\n1386\t    \"\"\"\n1387\t    Clear the global router cache.\n1388\t\n1389\t    This function resets the in-memory classification cache and statistics.\n1390\t    Primarily used for testing to ensure test isolation.\n1391\t\n1392\t    Example:\n1393\t        >>> clear_router_cache()\n1394\t        >>> router = get_router()\n1395\t        >>> classifications, timing = await router.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n1396\t        >>> assert intents → fetch + synthesize strands\n\"\"\"\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport time\nimport uuid\nimport re\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom logging import
