pipeline\"\n        assert classifications[1].utterance_fragment == \"check if it synced\"\n\n\n# --- 4. router → fetch command mapping -------------------------------------\n\nclass TestIntentToFetchCommandMapping:\n    \"\"\"The router maps each intent to the correct fetch source set.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES))\n    def test_map_intent_type_resolves_correctly(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"_map_intent_type maps router IntentType → fetch IntentType.\"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n        mapped = router._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        assert mapped == FetchIntentType(intent_type.value)\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES))\n    def test_fetch_command_source_set_matches_matrix(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"\n        For each fetchable intent, the source set returned by\n        get_fetch_commands matches the matrix (order-independent). A divergence\n        means an intent fetches the wrong — or no — sources.\n        \"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        specs = get_fetch_commands(mapped)\n        actual_sources = {spec.source for spec in specs}\n\n        assert actual_sources == EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES[intent_type], (\n            f\"Source set for {intent_type.value} diverged from expected\"\n        )\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_REQUIRED_SOURCES))\n    def test_required_sources_match_matrix(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"Required sources for each intent match the matrix's required=True set.\"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        required = set(get_required_sources(mapped))\n\n        assert required == EXPECTED_REQUIRED_SOURCES[intent_type], (\n            f\"Required sources for {intent_type.value} diverged from expected\"\n        )\n\n    def test_expected_sets_are_consistent_with_matrix_definition(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Guard against the EXPECTED_* dicts and FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX drifting\n        apart silently: every fetchable intent present in the matrix must have\n        an expected entry, and vice-versa.\n\n        Compared on .value strings because FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX is keyed by the\n        fetch IntentType while EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES is keyed by the router\n        IntentType — sibling enums that share value strings (the same contract\n        _map_intent_type relies on) but are distinct classes.\n        \"\"\"\n        matrix_values = {t.value for t in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX}\n        expected_values = {t.value for t in EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES}\n\n        # TASK_PROFILE/CLARIFICATION are intentionally absent from EXPECTED_*\n        # (TASK_PROFILE escalates instead of fetching; CLARIFICATION is a\n        # non-dispatched meta-type). Every *other* matrix intent must be covered.\n        assert expected_values == matrix_values, (\n            \"EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES does not cover exactly the fetch matrix\"\n        )\n\n    def test_action_intent_has_required_sources_others_optional(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        ACTION is the canonical 'required source' intent: its required sources\n        are a proper non-empty subset of its full source set, and the\n        orchestrator will treat their failure as blocking.\n        \"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(IntentType.ACTION)\n        full = {s.source for s in get_fetch_commands(mapped)}\n        required = set(get_required_sources(mapped))\n\n        assert required  # non-empty\n        assert required.issubset(full)  # required ⊆ full\n        assert FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS in required  # the action-specific source\n\n\n# --- 5. meta-type handling: TASK_PROFILE & CLARIFICATION --------------------\n\nclass TestMetaTypeHandling:\n    \"\"\"\n    TASK_PROFILE and CLARIFICATION are not dispatched to the fetch pipeline:\n    TASK_PROFILE escalates to a NEEDLE bead, CLARIFICATION awaits user input.\n    Neither should resolve fetch commands.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_task_profile_classifies_but_does_not_fetch(self, router):\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"task-profile\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"implement the digest feature\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.9,\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification], _ = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"implement the digest feature\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        # Classified correctly...\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE\n        # ...but its string value is not a key in the fetch matrix, so it would\n        # never resolve real fetch sources (process_intent escalates instead).\n        mapped = r._map_intent_type(IntentType.TASK_PROFILE)\n        # The default fallback is STATUS, proving TASK_PROFILE has no dedicated\n        # fetch mapping of its own.\n        assert mapped == FetchIntentType.STATUS\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_clarification_is_not_a_fetch_intent(self, router):\n        \"\"\"CLARIFICATION is a meta-type with no dedicated fetch mapping.\"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"what?\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.3,\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification], _ = await r.classify_utterance(\"what?\", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.CLARIFICATION\n        # Its value is absent from the fetch matrix.\n        assert classification.intent_type.value not in {\n            t.value for t in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX\n        }\n\n\n# --- 6. malformed-response fallback ----------------------------------------\n\nclass TestFallbackOnMalformedResponse:\n    \"\"\"A non-JSON LLM response must degrade to a single STATUS intent, not raise.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_unparseable_response_defaults_to_status(self, router):\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, \"this is not json at all\")\n\n        classifications = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"something happened\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        assert len(classifications) == 1\n        assert classifications[0].intent_type == IntentType.STATUS\n        assert classifications[0].confidence < 0.6  # flagged as low-confidence\n\n\n# --- 7. edge cases: empty/ambiguous inputs (bead adc-5qdx) ------------------\n#\n# The classes above cover the happy path for every IntentType plus malformed\n# responses. This class closes the edge-case gap called out in the acceptance\n# criteria — empty strings, whitespace-only input, zero-intent responses, and\n# stripped-down LLM payloads missing optional fields. The ZAI client is still\n# mocked, so each case is deterministic: we feed the exact JSON envelope the\n# router would receive for that degenerate input and assert it degrades safely\n# rather than raising.\n\n\nclass TestEdgeCases:\n    \"\"\"Empty, whitespace, and ambiguous inputs must classify without raising.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_empty_string_utterance_classifies_to_clarification(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        An empty utterance must flow through classify_utterance without raising.\n        A realistic LLM response for empty input is a single low-confidence\n        clarification; the router must reproduce that verdict.\n        \"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.2,\n            \"reasoning\": \"empty utterance, nothing to route\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\"\", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.CLARIFICATION\n        assert classification.utterance_fragment == \"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_whitespace_only_utterance_does_not_crash(self, router):\n        \"\"\"Whitespace-only input is no different structurally from empty.\"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.1,\n            \"reasoning\": \"whitespace only\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\"   \\n\\t  \", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.CLARIFICATION\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_empty_intent_array_returns_empty_list(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        If the LLM segments the utterance into zero intents (empty JSON array),\n        the router returns an empty list — not None, not a crash, not a\n        synthesized fallback. Callers must tolerate [].\n        \"\"\"\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, \"[]\")\n\n        classifications, _ = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"anything — model returned no intents\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        assert classifications == []\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_missing_optional_fields_use_defaults(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        The router must tolerate a minimal payload containing only intent_type.\n        Every optional field falls back to a defined default rather than\n        KeyError/AttributeError. Locks the .get()-with-default contract in\n        classify_utterance against drift in the IntentClassification dataclass.\n        \"\"\"\n        utterance = \"show me the deploy status\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\"intent_type\": \"status\"}])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification], _ = await r.classify_utterance(utterance, \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.STATUS\n        assert classification.project_slug is None\n        assert classification.urgency == \"normal\"\n        assert classification.reasoning == \"\"\n        # confidence default in the parser is 0.8 (NOT the dataclass's 1.0).\n        assert classification.confidence == pytest.approx(0.8)\n        # fragment falls back to the full utterance when the LLM omits it.\n        assert classification.utterance_fragment == utterance\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_deeply_ambiguous_fragment_routes_to_clarification(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        Per prompts/router.md, confidence < 0.7 routes to clarification. A\n        maximally ambiguous fragment (\"hmm\") yields a clarification intent with\n        sub-threshold confidence — the router must surface that meta-type intact\n        rather than forcing a best-guess dispatch.\n        \"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"hmm\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.35,\n            \"reasoning\": \"no actionable signal in the utterance\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\"hmm\", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.CLARIFICATION\n        assert classification.confidence < 0.7\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_missing_intent_type_field_defaults_to_status(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        An intent object with no intent_type key at all must fall back to STATUS\n        (the parser's documented default) rather than raising — mirrors the\n        unknown-string fallback but for the field-absent case.\n        \"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\"utterance_fragment\": \"something\"}])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\"something\", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.STATUS\n\n\n# --- 8. cache key generation (bead adc-4a3kd) ----------------------------------\n\nclass TestCacheKeyGeneration:\n    \"\"\"\n    Cache key generation ensures consistent hash values for identical inputs.\n    The generate_cache_key() function produces SHA256 hex digests for\n    utterance + optional context combinations.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_returns_32_char_hex_string(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Cache key must be a 32-character hexadecimal string (SHA256 hex digest).\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n        key = router.generate_cache_key(\"test utterance\", \"status\")\n\n        assert len(key) == 32\n        # Verify it's a valid hex string (only contains 0-9 and a-f)\n        assert all(c in \"0123456789abcdef\" for c in key)\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_consistent_for_same_inputs(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Same inputs must produce the same hash (deterministic behavior).\n        This is the core property that makes caching work.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n        utterance = \"check the pods in ardenone-manager\"\n        context = \"status\"\n\n        key1 = router.generate_cache_key(utterance, context)\n        key2 = router.generate_cache_key(utterance, context)\n\n        assert key1 == key2\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_different_for_different_utterances(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Different utterances must produce different hashes (no collisions).\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        key1 = router.generate_cache_key(\"first utterance\", \"status\")\n        key2 = router.generate_cache_key(\"second utterance\", \"status\")\n\n        assert key1 != key2\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_different_for_different_contexts(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Same utterance with different contexts must produce different hashes.\n        This ensures cache keys are context-sensitive.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n        utterance = \"check pods\"\n\n        key_status = router.generate_cache_key(utterance, \"status\")\n        key_action = router.generate_cache_key(utterance, \"action\")\n\n        assert key_status != key_action\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_handles_none_context_gracefully(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        None context must be treated as empty string (no crash).\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n        utterance = \"check pods\"\n\n        # Should not raise\n        key_with_none = router.generate_cache_key(utterance, None)\n        key_with_empty = router.generate_cache_key(utterance, \"\")\n\n        # None and empty string should produce the same hash\n        assert key_with_none == key_with_empty\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_handles_empty_string_context(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Empty string context must be handled gracefully.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        # Should not raise\n        key = router.generate_cache_key(\"utterance\", \"\")\n\n        assert len(key) == 32\n        assert all(c in \"0123456789abcdef\" for c in key)\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_delimiter_prevents_collisions(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        The delimiter ('|') must prevent hash collisions between:\n        - \"A\" + \"BC\" vs \"AB\" + \"C\"\n        Without proper delimiter, these would collide.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        key1 = router.generate_cache_key(\"A\", \"BC\")\n        key2 = router.generate_cache_key(\"AB\", \"C\")\n\n        assert key1 != key2\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_special_characters(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Special characters in utterance or context must not break hashing.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        # Test with various special characters\n        key = router.generate_cache_key(\n            \"check pods!@#$%^&*()\",\n            \"status-context-with-dashes\"\n        )\n\n        assert len(key) == 32\n        assert all(c in \"0123456789abcdef\" for c in key)\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_unicode_characters(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Unicode characters (emoji, non-ASCII) must be handled correctly.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        # Test with emoji and unicode\n        key = router.generate_cache_key(\"check pods 🚀\", \"状态-状态\")\n\n        assert len(key) == 32\n        assert all(c in \"0123456789abcdef\" for c in key)\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_long_inputs(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Very long utterances or contexts must hash correctly.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        long_utterance = \"check pods \" * 100  # ~1100 characters\n        long_context = \"status-context-\" * 50  # ~700 characters\n\n        key = router.generate_cache_key(long_utterance, long_context)\n\n        assert len(key) == 32\n        assert all(c in \"0123456789abcdef\" for c in key)\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_whitespace_sensitivity(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Whitespace differences must produce different hashes.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        key1 = router.generate_cache_key(\"check pods\", \"status\")\n        key2 = router.generate_cache_key(\"check  pods\", \"status\")  # double space\n        key3 = router.generate_cache_key(\"check pods \", \"status\")  # trailing space\n\n        assert key1 != key2\n        assert key1 != key3\n\n\n# --- 9. cache behavior (bead adc-2qhmb) ---------------------------------------\n\nclass TestRouterCacheBehavior:\n    \"\"\"\n    Router response caching eliminates redundant ZAI proxy calls for similar\n    utterances. Cache key: (utterance_hash, session_id), TTL: 5 minutes.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.fixture(autouse=True)\n    def clear_cache_before_each_test(self):\n        \"\"\"Clear the router cache before each test to ensure isolation.\"\"\"\n        from src.intent.router import clear_router_cache\n        clear_router_cache()\n        yield\n        # Also clear after the test\n        clear_router_cache()\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_hit_skips_zai_call(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        A cache hit returns cached classifications without calling the ZAI proxy.\n        The cached result includes all original classification fields.\n        \"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n            \"project_slug\": \"kalshi-tape\",\n            \"urgency\": \"high\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"check pods\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.95,\n            \"reasoning\": \"cluster health check\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        # First call: cache miss, calls ZAI\n        classifications1, timing1 = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"check pods\", \"session-123\"\n        )\n        assert len(classifications1) == 1\n        assert timing1[\"cached\"] is False\n        assert timing1[\"total_ms\"] > 0\n\n        # Second call: cache hit, no ZAI call\n        classifications2, timing2 = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"check pods\", \"session-123\"\n        )\n        assert len(classifications2) == 1\n        assert timing2[\"cached\"] is True\n        assert timing2[\"total_ms\"] == 0\n\n        # Cached result matches original\n        assert classifications2[0].intent_type == classifications1[0].intent_type\n        assert classifications2[0].project_slug == classifications1[0].project_slug\n        assert classifications2[0].urgency == classifications1[0].urgency\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_miss_for_different_utterance(self, router):\n        \"\"\"Different utterances produce cache misses and call ZAI each time.\"\"\"\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"first\",\n        }]))\n\n        await r.classify_utterance(\"first\", \"session-123\")\n\n        # Change the canned response for the second call\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"action\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"second\",\n        }]))\n\n        classifications2, timing2 = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"second\", \"session-123\"\n        )\n        assert timing2[\"cached\"] is False\n        assert classifications2[0].intent_type == IntentType.ACTION\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_key_includes_session_id(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        Cache key includes session_id: same utterance in different sessions\n        produces cache misses (different cache keys).\n        \"\"\"\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"check status\",\n        }]))\n\n        await r.classify_utterance(\"check status\", \"session-123\")\n        _, timing2 = await r.classify_utterance(\"check status\", \"session-456\")\n\n        assert timing2[\"cached\"] is False\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_hit_statistics_tracking(self, router):\n        \"\"\"Cache statistics track hits and misses correctly.\"\"\"\n        import src.intent.router as router_module\n\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"test\",\n        }]))\n\n        # Second call: hit\n        await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n        assert router_module._cache_hits == 1\n        assert router_module._cache_misses == 1\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_ttl_is_five_minutes(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        Cache TTL is 5 minutes (300 seconds). Entries older than TTL are treated\n        as misses and removed.\n        \"\"\"\n        import time\n        from unittest.mock import patch\n\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"test\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        # First call: populate cache\n        await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n\n        # Mock time.time() to simulate 301 seconds later (beyond 300s TTL)\n        with patch('time.time', return_value=time.time() + 301):\n            # Should be a cache miss due to TTL expiration\n            _, timing = await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n            assert timing[\"cached\"] is False\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_stats_logged_periodically(self, router, caplog):\n        \"\"\"\n        Cache statistics are logged every 50 requests (by default). TestIntentToFetchCommandMapping:\n    \"\"\"The router maps each intent to the correct fetch source set.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES))\n    def test_map_intent_type_resolves_correctly(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"_map_intent_type maps router IntentType → fetch IntentType.\"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n        mapped = router._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        assert mapped == FetchIntentType(intent_type.value)\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES))\n    def test_fetch_command_source_set_matches_matrix(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"\n        For each fetchable intent, the source set returned by\n        get_fetch_commands matches the matrix (order-independent). A divergence\n        means an intent fetches the wrong — or no — sources.\n        \"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        specs = get_fetch_commands(mapped)\n        actual_sources = {spec.source for spec in specs}\n\n        assert actual_sources == EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES[intent_type], (\n            f\"Source set for {intent_type.value} diverged from expected\"\n        )\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_REQUIRED_SOURCES))\n    def test_required_sources_match_matrix(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"Required sources for each intent match the matrix's required=True set.\"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        required = set(get_required_sources(mapped))\n\n        assert required == EXPECTED_REQUIRED_SOURCES[intent_type], (\n            f\"Required sources for {intent_type.value} diverged from expected\"\n        )\n\n    def test_expected_sets_are_consistent_with_matrix_definition(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Guard against the EXPECTED_* dicts and FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX drifting\n        apart silently: every fetchable intent present in the matrix must have\n        an expected entry, and vice-versa.\n\n        Compared on .value strings because FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX is keyed by the\n        fetch IntentType while EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES is keyed by the router\n        IntentType — sibling enums that share value strings (the same contract\n        _map_intent_type relies on) but are distinct classes.\n        \"\"\"\n        matrix_values = {t.value for t in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX}\n        expected_values = {t.value for t in EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES}\n\n        # TASK_PROFILE/CLARIFICATION are intentionally absent from EXPECTED_*\n        # (TASK_PROFILE escalates instead of fetching; CLARIFICATION is a\n        # non-dispatched meta-type). Every *other* matrix intent must be covered.\n        assert expected_values == matrix_values, (\n            \"EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES does not cover exactly the fetch matrix\"\n        )\n\n    def test_action_intent_has_required_sources_others_optional(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        ACTION is the canonical 'required source' intent: its required sources\n        are a proper non-empty subset of its full source set, and the\n        orchestrator will treat their failure as blocking.\n        \"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(IntentType.ACTION)\n        full = {s.source for s in get_fetch_commands(mapped)}\n        required = set(get_required_sources(mapped))\n\n        assert required  # non-empty\n        assert required.issubset(full)  # required ⊆ full\n        assert FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS in required  # the action-specific source\n\n\n# --- 5. meta-type handling: TASK_PROFILE & CLARIFICATION --------------------\n\nclass TestMetaTypeHandling:\n    \"\"\"\n    TASK_PROFILE and CLARIFICATION are not dispatched to the fetch pipeline:\n    TASK_PROFILE escalates to a NEEDLE bead, CLARIFICATION awaits user input.\n    Neither should resolve fetch commands.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_task_profile_classifies_but_does_not_fetch(self, router):\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"task-profile\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"implement the digest feature\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.9,\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification], _ = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"implement the digest feature\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        # Classified correctly...\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE\n        # ...but its string value is not a key in the fetch matrix, so it would\n        # never resolve real fetch sources (process_intent escalates instead).\n        mapped = r._map_intent_type(IntentType.TASK_PROFILE)\n        # The default fallback is STATUS, proving TASK_PROFILE has no dedicated\n        # fetch mapping of its own.\n        assert mapped == FetchIntentType.STATUS\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_clarification_is_not_a_fetch_intent(self, router):\n        \"\"\"CLARIFICATION is a meta-type with no dedicated fetch mapping.\"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"what?\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.3,\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification], _ = await r.classify_utterance(\"what?\", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.CLARIFICATION\n        # Its value is absent from the fetch matrix.\n        assert classification.intent_type.value not in {\n            t.value for t in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX\n        }\n\n\n# --- 6. malformed-response fallback ----------------------------------------\n\nclass TestFallbackOnMalformedResponse:\n    \"\"\"A non-JSON LLM response must degrade to a single STATUS intent, not raise.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_unparseable_response_defaults_to_status(self, router):\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, \"this is not json at all\")\n\n        classifications = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"something happened\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        assert len(classifications) == 1\n        assert classifications[0].intent_type == IntentType.STATUS\n        assert classifications[0].confidence < 0.6  # flagged as low-confidence\n\n\n# --- 7. edge cases: empty/ambiguous inputs (bead adc-5qdx) ------------------\n#\n# The classes above cover the happy path for every IntentType plus malformed\n# responses. This class closes the edge-case gap called out in the acceptance\n# criteria — empty strings, whitespace-only input, zero-intent responses, and\n# stripped-down LLM payloads missing optional fields. The ZAI client is still\n# mocked, so each case is deterministic: we feed the exact JSON envelope the\n# router would receive for that degenerate input and assert it degrades safely\n# rather than raising.\n\n\nclass TestEdgeCases:\n    \"\"\"Empty, whitespace, and ambiguous inputs must classify without raising.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_empty_string_utterance_classifies_to_clarification(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        An empty utterance must flow through classify_utterance without raising.\n        A realistic LLM response for empty input is a single low-confidence\n        clarification; the router must reproduce that verdict.\n        \"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.2,\n            \"reasoning\": \"empty utterance, nothing to route\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\"\", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.CLARIFICATION\n        assert classification.utterance_fragment == \"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_whitespace_only_utterance_does_not_crash(self, router):\n        \"\"\"Whitespace-only input is no different structurally from empty.\"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.1,\n            \"reasoning\": \"whitespace only\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\"   \\n\\t  \", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.CLARIFICATION\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_empty_intent_array_returns_empty_list(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        If the LLM segments the utterance into zero intents (empty JSON array),\n        the router returns an empty list — not None, not a crash, not a\n        synthesized fallback. Callers must tolerate [].\n        \"\"\"\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, \"[]\")\n\n        classifications, _ = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"anything — model returned no intents\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        assert classifications == []\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_missing_optional_fields_use_defaults(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        The router must tolerate a minimal payload containing only intent_type.\n        Every optional field falls back to a defined default rather than\n        KeyError/AttributeError. Locks the .get()-with-default contract in\n        classify_utterance against drift in the IntentClassification dataclass.\n        \"\"\"\n        utterance = \"show me the deploy status\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\"intent_type\": \"status\"}])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification], _ = await r.classify_utterance(utterance, \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.STATUS\n        assert classification.project_slug is None\n        assert classification.urgency == \"normal\"\n        assert classification.reasoning == \"\"\n        # confidence default in the parser is 0.8 (NOT the dataclass's 1.0).\n        assert classification.confidence == pytest.approx(0.8)\n        # fragment falls back to the full utterance when the LLM omits it.\n        assert classification.utterance_fragment == utterance\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_deeply_ambiguous_fragment_routes_to_clarification(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        Per prompts/router.md, confidence < 0.7 routes to clarification. A\n        maximally ambiguous fragment (\"hmm\") yields a clarification intent with\n        sub-threshold confidence — the router must surface that meta-type intact\n        rather than forcing a best-guess dispatch.\n        \"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"hmm\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.35,\n            \"reasoning\": \"no actionable signal in the utterance\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\"hmm\", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.CLARIFICATION\n        assert classification.confidence < 0.7\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_missing_intent_type_field_defaults_to_status(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        An intent object with no intent_type key at all must fall back to STATUS\n        (the parser's documented default) rather than raising — mirrors the\n        unknown-string fallback but for the field-absent case.\n        \"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\"utterance_fragment\": \"something\"}])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\"something\", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.STATUS\n\n\n# --- 8. cache key generation (bead adc-4a3kd) ----------------------------------\n\nclass TestCacheKeyGeneration:\n    \"\"\"\n    Cache key generation ensures consistent hash values for identical inputs.\n    The generate_cache_key() function produces SHA256 hex digests for\n    utterance + optional context combinations.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_returns_64_char_hex_string(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Cache key must be a 64-character hexadecimal string (SHA256 hex digest).\n        SHA256 produces 256 bits = 32 bytes = 64 hex characters.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n        key = router.generate_cache_key(\"test utterance\", \"status\")\n\n        assert len(key) == 64\n        # Verify it's a valid hex string (only contains 0-9 and a-f)\n        assert all(c in \"0123456789abcdef\" for c in key)\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_consistent_for_same_inputs(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Same inputs must produce the same hash (deterministic behavior).\n        This is the core property that makes caching work.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n        utterance = \"check the pods in ardenone-manager\"\n        context = \"status\"\n\n        key1 = router.generate_cache_key(utterance, context)\n        key2 = router.generate_cache_key(utterance, context)\n\n        assert key1 == key2\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_different_for_different_utterances(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Different utterances must produce different hashes (no collisions).\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        key1 = router.generate_cache_key(\"first utterance\", \"status\")\n        key2 = router.generate_cache_key(\"second utterance\", \"status\")\n\n        assert key1 != key2\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_different_for_different_contexts(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Same utterance with different contexts must produce different hashes.\n        This ensures cache keys are context-sensitive.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n        utterance = \"check pods\"\n\n        key_status = router.generate_cache_key(utterance, \"status\")\n        key_action = router.generate_cache_key(utterance, \"action\")\n\n        assert key_status != key_action\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_handles_none_context_gracefully(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        None context must be treated as empty string (no crash).\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n        utterance = \"check pods\"\n\n        # Should not raise\n        key_with_none = router.generate_cache_key(utterance, None)\n        key_with_empty = router.generate_cache_key(utterance, \"\")\n\n        # None and empty string should produce the same hash\n        assert key_with_none == key_with_empty\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_handles_empty_string_context(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Empty string context must be handled gracefully.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        # Should not raise\n        key = router.generate_cache_key(\"utterance\", \"\")\n\n        assert len(key) == 32\n        assert all(c in \"0123456789abcdef\" for c in key)\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_delimiter_prevents_collisions(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        The delimiter ('|') must prevent hash collisions between:\n        - \"A\" + \"BC\" vs \"AB\" + \"C\"\n        Without proper delimiter, these would collide.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        key1 = router.generate_cache_key(\"A\", \"BC\")\n        key2 = router.generate_cache_key(\"AB\", \"C\")\n\n        assert key1 != key2\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_special_characters(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Special characters in utterance or context must not break hashing.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        # Test with various special characters\n        key = router.generate_cache_key(\n            \"check pods!@#$%^&*()\",\n            \"status-context-with-dashes\"\n        )\n\n        assert len(key) == 32\n        assert all(c in \"0123456789abcdef\" for c in key)\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_unicode_characters(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Unicode characters (emoji, non-ASCII) must be handled correctly.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        # Test with emoji and unicode\n        key = router.generate_cache_key(\"check pods 🚀\", \"状态-状态\")\n\n        assert len(key) == 32\n        assert all(c in \"0123456789abcdef\" for c in key)\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_long_inputs(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Very long utterances or contexts must hash correctly.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        long_utterance = \"check pods \" * 100  # ~1100 characters\n        long_context = \"status-context-\" * 50  # ~700 characters\n\n        key = router.generate_cache_key(long_utterance, long_context)\n\n        assert len(key) == 32\n        assert all(c in \"0123456789abcdef\" for c in key)\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_whitespace_sensitivity(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Whitespace differences must produce different hashes.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        key1 = router.generate_cache_key(\"check pods\", \"status\")\n        key2 = router.generate_cache_key(\"check  pods\", \"status\")  # double space\n        key3 = router.generate_cache_key(\"check pods \", \"status\")  # trailing space\n\n        assert key1 != key2\n        assert key1 != key3\n\n\n# --- 9. cache behavior (bead adc-2qhmb) ---------------------------------------\n\nclass TestRouterCacheBehavior:\n    \"\"\"\n    Router response caching eliminates redundant ZAI proxy calls for similar\n    utterances. Cache key: (utterance_hash, session_id), TTL: 5 minutes.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.fixture(autouse=True)\n    def clear_cache_before_each_test(self):\n        \"\"\"Clear the router cache before each test to ensure isolation.\"\"\"\n        from src.intent.router import clear_router_cache\n        clear_router_cache()\n        yield\n        # Also clear after the test\n        clear_router_cache()\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_hit_skips_zai_call(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        A cache hit returns cached classifications without calling the ZAI proxy.\n        The cached result includes all original classification fields.\n        \"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n            \"project_slug\": \"kalshi-tape\",\n            \"urgency\": \"high\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"check pods\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.95,\n            \"reasoning\": \"cluster health check\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        # First call: cache miss, calls ZAI\n        classifications1, timing1 = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"check pods\", \"session-123\"\n        )\n        assert len(classifications1) == 1\n        assert timing1[\"cached\"] is False\n        assert timing1[\"total_ms\"] > 0\n\n        # Second call: cache hit, no ZAI call\n        classifications2, timing2 = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"check pods\", \"session-123\"\n        )\n        assert len(classifications2) == 1\n        assert timing2[\"cached\"] is True\n        assert timing2[\"total_ms\"] == 0\n\n        # Cached result matches original\n        assert classifications2[0].intent_type == classifications1[0].intent_type\n        assert classifications2[0].project_slug == classifications1[0].project_slug\n        assert classifications2[0].urgency == classifications1[0].urgency\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_miss_for_different_utterance(self, router):\n        \"\"\"Different utterances produce cache misses and call ZAI each time.\"\"\"\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"first\",\n        }]))\n\n        await r.classify_utterance(\"first\", \"session-123\")\n\n        # Change the canned response for the second call\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"action\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"second\",\n        }]))\n\n        classifications2, timing2 = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"second\", \"session-123\"\n        )\n        assert timing2[\"cached\"] is False\n        assert classifications2[0].intent_type == IntentType.ACTION\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_key_includes_session_id(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        Cache key includes session_id: same utterance in different sessions\n        produces cache misses (different cache keys).\n        \"\"\"\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"check status\",\n        }]))\n\n        await r.classify_utterance(\"check status\", \"session-123\")\n        _, timing2 = await r.classify_utterance(\"check status\", \"session-456\")\n\n        assert timing2[\"cached\"] is False\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_hit_statistics_tracking(self, router):\n        \"\"\"Cache statistics track hits and misses correctly.\"\"\"\n        import src.intent.router as router_module\n\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"test\",\n        }]))\n\n        # First call: miss (cache was cleared by fixture, so stats start at 0)\n        await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n        assert router_module._cache_misses == 1\n        assert router_module._cache_hits == 0\n\n        # Second call: hit\n        await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n        assert router_module._cache_hits == 1\n        assert router_module._cache_misses == 1\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_ttl_is_five_minutes(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        Cache TTL is 5 minutes (300 seconds). Entries older than TTL are treated\n        as misses and removed.\n        \"\"\"\n        import time\n        from unittest.mock import patch\n\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"test\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        # First call: populate cache\n        await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n\n        # Mock time.time() to simulate 301 seconds later (beyond 300s TTL)\n        with patch('time.time', return_value=time.time() + 301):\n            # Should be a cache miss due to TTL expiration\n            _, timing = await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n            assert timing[\"cached\"] is False\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_stats_logged_periodically(self, router, caplog):\n        \"\"\"\n        Cache statistics are logged every 50 requests (by default). This test\n        verifies the periodic logging behavior.\n        \"\"\"\n        import logging\n        caplog.set_level(logging.INFO)\n\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"test\",\n        }]))\n\n        import src.intent.router as router_module\n\n        # Set stats so next request triggers logging (49 hits so far)\n        router_module._cache_hits = 49\n        router_module._cache_misses = 0\n\n        # This 50th request should trigger stats logging\n        with caplog.at_level(logging.INFO):\n            await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n\n        # Verify stats were logged (look for the stats log message)\n        assert any(\"Router cache statistics\" in record.message for record in caplog.records)\n        assert any(\"hit_rate=\" in record.message for record in caplog.records)\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":744,"oldLines":7,"newStart":744,"newLines":7,"lines":["         # Should not raise","         key = router.generate_cache_key(\"utterance\", \"\")"," ","-        assert len(key) == 32","+        assert len(key) == 64","         assert all(c in \"0123456789abcdef\" for c in key)"," ","     def test_generate_cache_key_delimiter_prevents_collisions(self):"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

Mirrors required=True in the matrix.\nEXPECTED_REQUIRED_SOURCES = {\n    IntentType.STATUS: set(),\n    IntentType.ACTION: {\n        FetchSource.KUBECTL_PODS, FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS,\n        FetchSource.ARGOCD_APP,\n    },\n    IntentType.BRAINSTORM: set(),\n    IntentType.LOOKUP: set(),\n    IntentType.REMINDER: {FetchSource.REMINDERS},\n    IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: {FetchSource.SESSION_STATE},\n    IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: {FetchSource.COMPONENTS},\n}\n\n\n# --- fixtures ---------------------------------------------------------------\n\nROUTER_MD_STUB = \"\"\"\\\n# Intent Router System Prompt (test stub)\n\nReturn ONLY a JSON array of intent objects.\n\"\"\"\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef temp_router_md():\n    \"\"\"A throwaway router.md so the router never reads the production prompt.\"\"\"\n    with NamedTemporaryFile(mode=\"w\", suffix=\".md\", delete=False) as f:\n        f.write(ROUTER_MD_STUB)\n        path = f.name\n    yield path\n    Path(path).unlink(missing_ok=True)\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef temp_urgency_md():\n    \"\"\"A throwaway urgency.md so _build_system_prompt stays hermetic.\"\"\"\n    with NamedTemporaryFile(mode=\"w\", suffix=\".md\", delete=False) as f:\n        f.write(\"# Urgency (test stub)\\n\")\n        path = f.name\n    yield path\n    Path(path).unlink(missing_ok=True)\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef router(temp_router_md, temp_urgency_md):\n    \"\"\"\n    An IntentRouter pointed at the stub router.md, with a hermetic store\n    (get_session -> None, so no session context is appended) and a registered\n    urgency prompt. The ZAI client is injected per-test by the caller.\n    \"\"\"\n    reload_manager = HotReloadManager()\n    reload_manager.register_prompt(\"urgency\", temp_urgency_md)\n\n    r = IntentRouter(prompt_path=Path(temp_router_md))\n    r._reload_manager = reload_manager\n\n    store = MagicMock()\n    store.get_session = AsyncMock(return_value=None)\n    r.store = store\n    return r\n\n\ndef _make_router_with_response(router, response_text):\n    \"\"\"Wire a mock ZAI client onto the router that returns response_text.\"\"\"\n    mock_client = AsyncMock()\n    # The router expects call_simple to return a dict with content and timing\n    mock_response = {\n        \"content\": response_text,\n        \"timing_network_ms\": 100.0,\n        \"timing_inference_ms\": 200.0,\n    }\n    mock_client.call_simple = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_response)\n    router._zai_client = mock_client\n    router._router_zai_client = mock_client  # Also mock the dedicated router client\n    return router\n\n\n# --- 1. classification parsing ---------------------------------------------\n\nclass TestIntentClassification:\n    \"\"\"Each pre-canned utterance classifies to its pipeline\"\n        assert classifications[1].utterance_fragment == \"check if it synced\"\n\n\n# --- 4. router → fetch command mapping -------------------------------------\n\nclass TestIntentToFetchCommandMapping:\n    \"\"\"The router maps each intent to the correct fetch source set.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES))\n    def test_map_intent_type_resolves_correctly(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"_map_intent_type maps router IntentType → fetch IntentType.\"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n        mapped = router._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        assert mapped == FetchIntentType(intent_type.value)\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES))\n    def test_fetch_command_source_set_matches_matrix(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"\n        For each fetchable intent, the source set returned by\n        get_fetch_commands matches the matrix (order-independent). A divergence\n        means an intent fetches the wrong — or no — sources.\n        \"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        specs = get_fetch_commands(mapped)\n        actual_sources = {spec.source for spec in specs}\n\n        assert actual_sources == EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES[intent_type], (\n            f\"Source set for {intent_type.value} diverged from expected\"\n        )\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_REQUIRED_SOURCES))\n    def test_required_sources_match_matrix(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"Required sources for each intent match the matrix's required=True set.\"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        required = set(get_required_sources(mapped))\n\n        assert required == EXPECTED_REQUIRED_SOURCES[intent_type], (\n            f\"Required sources for {intent_type.value} diverged from expected\"\n        )\n\n    def test_expected_sets_are_consistent_with_matrix_definition(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Guard against the EXPECTED_* dicts and FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX drifting\n        apart silently: every fetchable intent present in the matrix must have\n        an expected entry, and vice-versa.\n\n        Compared on .value strings because FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX is keyed by the\n        fetch IntentType while EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES is keyed by the router\n        IntentType — sibling enums that share value strings (the same contract\n        _map_intent_type relies on) but are distinct classes.\n        \"\"\"\n        matrix_values = {t.value for t in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX}\n        expected_values = {t.value for t in EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES}\n\n        # TASK_PROFILE/CLARIFICATION are intentionally absent from EXPECTED_*\n        # (TASK_PROFILE escalates instead of fetching; CLARIFICATION is a\n        # non-dispatched meta-type). Every *other* matrix intent must be covered.\n        assert expected_values == matrix_values, (\n            \"EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES does not cover exactly the fetch matrix\"\n        )\n\n    def test_action_intent_has_required_sources_others_optional(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        ACTION is the canonical 'required source' intent: its required sources\n        are a proper non-empty subset of its full source set, and the\n        orchestrator will treat their failure as blocking.\n        \"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(IntentType.ACTION)\n        full = {s.source for s in get_fetch_commands(mapped)}\n        required = set(get_required_sources(mapped))\n\n        assert required  # non-empty\n        assert required.issubset(full)  # required ⊆ full\n        assert FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS in required  # the action-specific source\n\n\n# --- 5. meta-type handling: TASK_PROFILE & CLARIFICATION --------------------\n\nclass TestMetaTypeHandling:\n    \"\"\"\n    TASK_PROFILE and CLARIFICATION are not dispatched to the fetch pipeline:\n    TASK_PROFILE escalates to a NEEDLE bead, CLARIFICATION awaits user input.\n    Neither should resolve fetch commands.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_task_profile_classifies_but_does_not_fetch(self, router):\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"task-profile\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"implement the digest feature\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.9,\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification], _ = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"implement the digest feature\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        # Classified correctly...\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE\n        # ...but its string value is not a key in the fetch matrix, so it would\n        # never resolve real fetch sources (process_intent escalates instead).\n        mapped = r._map_intent_type(IntentType.TASK_PROFILE)\n        # The default fallback is STATUS, proving TASK_PROFILE has no dedicated\n        # fetch mapping of its own.\n        assert mapped == FetchIntentType.STATUS\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_clarification_is_not_a_fetch_intent(self, router):\n        \"\"\"CLARIFICATION is a meta-type with no dedicated fetch mapping.\"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"what?\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.3,\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification], _ = await r.classify_utterance(\"what?\", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.CLARIFICATION\n        # Its value is absent from the fetch matrix.\n        assert classification.intent_type.value not in {\n            t.value for t in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX\n        }\n\n\n# --- 6. malformed-response fallback ----------------------------------------\n\nclass TestFallbackOnMalformedResponse:\n    \"\"\"A non-JSON LLM response must degrade to a single STATUS intent, not raise.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_unparseable_response_defaults_to_status(self, router):\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, \"this is not json at all\")\n\n        classifications = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"something happened\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        assert len(classifications) == 1\n        assert classifications[0].intent_type == IntentType.STATUS\n        assert classifications[0].confidence < 0.6  # flagged as low-confidence\n\n\n# --- 7. edge cases: empty/ambiguous inputs (bead adc-5qdx) ------------------\n#\n# The classes above cover the happy path for every IntentType plus malformed\n# responses. This class closes the edge-case gap called out in the acceptance\n# criteria — empty strings, whitespace-only input, zero-intent responses, and\n# stripped-down LLM payloads missing optional fields. The ZAI client is still\n# mocked, so each case is deterministic: we feed the exact JSON envelope the\n# router would receive for that degenerate input and assert it degrades safely\n# rather than raising.\n\n\nclass TestEdgeCases:\n    \"\"\"Empty, whitespace, and ambiguous inputs must classify without raising.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_empty_string_utterance_classifies_to_clarification(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        An empty utterance must flow through classify_utterance without raising.\n        A realistic LLM response for empty input is a single low-confidence\n        clarification; the router must reproduce that verdict.\n        \"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.2,\n            \"reasoning\": \"empty utterance, nothing to route\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\"\", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.CLARIFICATION\n        assert classification.utterance_fragment == \"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_whitespace_only_utterance_does_not_crash(self, router):\n        \"\"\"Whitespace-only input is no different structurally from empty.\"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.1,\n            \"reasoning\": \"whitespace only\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\"   \\n\\t  \", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.CLARIFICATION\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_empty_intent_array_returns_empty_list(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        If the LLM segments the utterance into zero intents (empty JSON array),\n        the router returns an empty list — not None, not a crash, not a\n        synthesized fallback. Callers must tolerate [].\n        \"\"\"\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, \"[]\")\n\n        classifications, _ = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"anything — model returned no intents\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        assert classifications == []\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_missing_optional_fields_use_defaults(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        The router must tolerate a minimal payload containing only intent_type.\n        Every optional field falls back to a defined default rather than\n        KeyError/AttributeError. Locks the .get()-with-default contract in\n        classify_utterance against drift in the IntentClassification dataclass.\n        \"\"\"\n        utterance = \"show me the deploy status\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\"intent_type\": \"status\"}])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification], _ = await r.classify_utterance(utterance, \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.STATUS\n        assert classification.project_slug is None\n        assert classification.urgency == \"normal\"\n        assert classification.reasoning == \"\"\n        # confidence default in the parser is 0.8 (NOT the dataclass's 1.0).\n        assert classification.confidence == pytest.approx(0.8)\n        # fragment falls back to the full utterance when the LLM omits it.\n        assert classification.utterance_fragment == utterance\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_deeply_ambiguous_fragment_routes_to_clarification(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        Per prompts/router.md, confidence < 0.7 routes to clarification. A\n        maximally ambiguous fragment (\"hmm\") yields a clarification intent with\n        sub-threshold confidence — the router must surface that meta-type intact\n        rather than forcing a best-guess dispatch.\n        \"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"hmm\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.35,\n            \"reasoning\": \"no actionable signal in the utterance\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\"hmm\", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.CLARIFICATION\n        assert classification.confidence < 0.7\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_missing_intent_type_field_defaults_to_status(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        An intent object with no intent_type key at all must fall back to STATUS\n        (the parser's documented default) rather than raising — mirrors the\n        unknown-string fallback but for the field-absent case.\n        \"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\"utterance_fragment\": classification.intent_type == IntentType.STATUS\n\n\n# --- 8. cache key generation (bead adc-4a3kd) ----------------------------------\n\nclass TestCacheKeyGeneration:\n    \"\"\"\n    Cache key generation ensures consistent hash values for identical inputs.\n    The generate_cache_key() function produces SHA256 hex digests for\n    utterance + optional context combinations.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_returns_64_char_hex_string(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Cache key must be a 64-character hexadecimal string (SHA256 hex digest).\n        SHA256 produces 256 bits = 32 bytes = 64 hex characters.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n        key = router.generate_cache_key(\"test utterance\", \"status\")\n\n        assert len(key) == 64\n        # Verify it's a valid hex string (only contains 0-9 and a-f)\n        assert all(c in \"0123456789abcdef\" for c in key)\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_consistent_for_same_inputs(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Same inputs must produce the same hash (deterministic behavior).\n        This is the core property that makes caching work.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n        utterance = \"check the pods in ardenone-manager\"\n        context = \"status\"\n\n        key1 = router.generate_cache_key(utterance, context)\n        key2 = router.generate_cache_key(utterance, context)\n\n        assert key1 == key2\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_different_for_different_utterances(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Different utterances must produce different hashes (no collisions).\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        key1 = router.generate_cache_key(\"first utterance\", \"status\")\n        key2 = router.generate_cache_key(\"second utterance\", \"status\")\n\n        assert key1 != key2\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_different_for_different_contexts(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Same utterance with different contexts must produce different hashes.\n        This ensures cache keys are context-sensitive.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n        utterance = \"check pods\"\n\n        key_status = router.generate_cache_key(utterance, \"status\")\n        key_action = router.generate_cache_key(utterance, \"action\")\n\n        assert key_status != key_action\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_handles_none_context_gracefully(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        None context must be treated as empty string (no crash).\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n        utterance = \"check pods\"\n\n        # Should not raise\n        key_with_none = router.generate_cache_key(utterance, None)\n        key_with_empty = router.generate_cache_key(utterance, \"\")\n\n        # None and empty string should produce the same hash\n        assert key_with_none == key_with_empty\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_handles_empty_string_context(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Empty string context must be handled gracefully.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        # Should not raise\n        key = router.generate_cache_key(\"utterance\", \"\")\n\n        assert len(key) == 64\n        assert all(c in \"0123456789abcdef\" for c in key)\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_delimiter_prevents_collisions(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        The delimiter ('|') must prevent hash collisions between:\n        - \"A\" + \"BC\" vs \"AB\" + \"C\"\n        Without proper delimiter, these would collide.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        key1 = router.generate_cache_key(\"A\", \"BC\")\n        key2 = router.generate_cache_key(\"AB\", \"C\")\n\n        assert key1 != key2\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_special_characters(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Special characters in utterance or context must not break hashing.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        # Test with various special characters\n        key = router.generate_cache_key(\n            \"check pods!@#$%^&*()\",\n            \"status-context-with-dashes\"\n        )\n\n        assert len(key) == 32\n        assert all(c in \"0123456789abcdef\" for c in key)\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_unicode_characters(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Unicode characters (emoji, non-ASCII) must be handled correctly.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        # Test with emoji and unicode\n        key = router.generate_cache_key(\"check pods 🚀\", \"状态-状态\")\n\n        assert len(key) == 32\n        assert all(c in \"0123456789abcdef\" for c in key)\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_long_inputs(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Very long utterances or contexts must hash correctly.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        long_utterance = \"check pods \" * 100  # ~1100 characters\n        long_context = \"status-context-\" * 50  # ~700 characters\n\n        key = router.generate_cache_key(long_utterance, long_context)\n\n        assert len(key) == 32\n        assert all(c in \"0123456789abcdef\" for c in key)\n\n    def test_generate_cache_key_whitespace_sensitivity(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Whitespace differences must produce different hashes.\n        \"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n\n        key1 = router.generate_cache_key(\"check pods\", \"status\")\n        key2 = router.generate_cache_key(\"check  pods\", \"status\")  # double space\n        key3 = router.generate_cache_key(\"check pods \", \"status\")  # trailing space\n\n        assert key1 != key2\n        assert key1 != key3\n\n\n# --- 9. cache behavior (bead adc-2qhmb) ---------------------------------------\n\nclass TestRouterCacheBehavior:\n    \"\"\"\n    Router response caching eliminates redundant ZAI proxy calls for similar\n    utterances. Cache key: (utterance_hash, session_id), TTL: 5 minutes.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.fixture(autouse=True)\n    def clear_cache_before_each_test(self):\n        \"\"\"Clear the router cache before each test to ensure isolation.\"\"\"\n        from src.intent.router import clear_router_cache\n        clear_router_cache()\n        yield\n        # Also clear after the test\n        clear_router_cache()\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_hit_skips_zai_call(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        A cache hit returns cached classifications without calling the ZAI proxy.\n        The cached result includes all original classification fields.\n        \"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n            \"project_slug\": \"kalshi-tape\",\n            \"urgency\": \"high\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"check pods\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.95,\n            \"reasoning\": \"cluster health check\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        # First call: cache miss, calls ZAI\n        classifications1, timing1 = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"check pods\", \"session-123\"\n        )\n        assert len(classifications1) == 1\n        assert timing1[\"cached\"] is False\n        assert timing1[\"total_ms\"] > 0\n\n        # Second call: cache hit, no ZAI call\n        classifications2, timing2 = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"check pods\", \"session-123\"\n        )\n        assert len(classifications2) == 1\n        assert timing2[\"cached\"] is True\n        assert timing2[\"total_ms\"] == 0\n\n        # Cached result matches original\n        assert classifications2[0].intent_type == classifications1[0].intent_type\n        assert classifications2[0].project_slug == classifications1[0].project_slug\n        assert classifications2[0].urgency == classifications1[0].urgency\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_miss_for_different_utterance(self, router):\n        \"\"\"Different utterances produce cache misses and call ZAI each time.\"\"\"\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"first\",\n        }]))\n\n        await r.classify_utterance(\"first\", \"session-123\")\n\n        # Change the canned response for the second call\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"action\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"second\",\n        }]))\n\n        classifications2, timing2 = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"second\", \"session-123\"\n        )\n        assert timing2[\"cached\"] is False\n        assert classifications2[0].intent_type == IntentType.ACTION\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_key_includes_session_id(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        Cache key includes session_id: same utterance in different sessions\n        produces cache misses (different cache keys).\n        \"\"\"\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"check status\",\n        }]))\n\n        await r.classify_utterance(\"check status\", \"session-123\")\n        _, timing2 = await r.classify_utterance(\"check status\", \"session-456\")\n\n        assert timing2[\"cached\"] is False\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_hit_statistics_tracking(self, router):\n        \"\"\"Cache statistics track hits and misses correctly.\"\"\"\n        import src.intent.router as router_module\n\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"test\",\n        }]))\n\n        # First call: miss (cache was cleared by fixture, so stats start at 0)\n        await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n        assert router_module._cache_misses == 1\n        assert router_module._cache_hits == 0\n\n        # Second call: hit\n        await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n        assert router_module._cache_hits == 1\n        assert router_module._cache_misses == 1\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_ttl_is_five_minutes(self, router):\n        \"\"\"\n        Cache TTL is 5 minutes (300 seconds). Entries older than TTL are treated\n        as misses and removed.\n        \"\"\"\n        import time\n        from unittest.mock import patch\n\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"test\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        # First call: populate cache\n        await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n\n        # Mock time.time() to simulate 301 seconds later (beyond 300s TTL)\n        with patch('time.time', return_value=time.time() + 301):\n            # Should be a cache miss due to TTL expiration\n            _, timing = await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n            assert timing[\"cached\"] is False\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cache_stats_logged_periodically(self, router, caplog):\n        \"\"\"\n        Cache statistics are logged every 50 requests (by default). This test\n        verifies the periodic logging behavior.\n        \"\"\"\n        import logging\n        caplog.set_level(logging.INFO)\n\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"test\",\n        }]))\n\n        import src.intent.router as router_module\n\n        # Set stats so next request triggers logging (49 hits so far)\n        router_module._cache_hits = 49\n        router_module._cache_misses = 0\n\n        # This 50th request should trigger stats logging\n        with caplog.at_level(logging.INFO):\n            await r.classify_utterance(\"test\", \"session-1\")\n\n        # Verify stats were logged (look for the stats log message)\n        assert any(\"Router cache statistics\" in record.message for record in caplog.records)\n        assert any(\"hit_rate=\" in record.message for record in caplog.records)\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":772,"oldLines":7,"newStart":772,"newLines":7,"lines":["             \"status-context-with-dashes\"","         )"," ","-        assert len(key) == 32","+        assert len(key) == 64","         assert all(c in \"0123456789abcdef\" for c in key)"," ","     def test_generate_cache_key_unicode_characters(self):"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

  \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"remind me to check the deploy at 5pm\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.93,\n            \"reasoning\": \"scheduling a reminder\",\n        }]),\n        IntentType.REMINDER,\n    ),\n    (\n        \"add a new intent type for incident triage\",\n        json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"self-modification\",\n            \"project_slug\": \"aide-de-camp\",\n            \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"add a new intent type for incident triage\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.9,\n            \"reasoning\": \"instruction to modify the interface itself\",\n        }]),\n        IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n    ),\n    (\n        \"set up an alert when pod restarts exceed 5\",\n        json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"monitoring-config\",\n            \"project_slug\": None,\n            \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"set up an alert when pod restarts exceed 5\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.87,\n            \"reasoning\": \"configuring an ambient monitoring rule\",\n        }]),\n        IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n    ),\n    (\n        \"implement a Telegram digest feature for the canvas\",\n        json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"task-profile\",\n            \"project_slug\": \"aide-de-camp\",\n
